---
title: "Day 4: AI in the Physical World"
author: "Simon Guest"
format:
  revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    incremental: true
    center-title-slide: true
    embed-resources: true
    theme: [default, ../../theme/dark.scss]
    logo: ../../theme/codercub.jpg
    highlight-style: dracula
    title-slide-attributes:
      data-background-gradient: "linear-gradient(135deg, #0a0e1a 0%, #0d2137 60%, #1a1f35 100%)"

---

## Recap

- What did we learn?

## Exploring Patterns in Data

- Bring in diagrams from https://microbit.org/teach/lessons/exploring-patterns-in-data/

# What is a micro:bit?

## What is a micro:bit?

![](images/microbit-front.png){fig-align="center"}

## What is a micro:bit?

![](images/microbit-back.png){fig-align="center"}

## What is an Accelerometer?

- A small chip that measures acceleration
  - How fast moves from one speed to another
- Measures acceleration in 3 directions: X, Y, and Z
  - Measures both positive and negative direction

## What is an Accelerometer?

![](images/accelerometer.jpg){fig-align="center"}

# *If the micro:bit is plugged in, how do we move it around?*

## Introducing the micro:bit Wearable!

![](images/wearable.jpg){fig-align="center"}

# Our Plan

## Our Plan

- Setup and connect the micro:bit
- Collect accelerometer data
- Use this data to train a model
- Test our model
- (Tomorrow) Use the model in code

## Walkthrough / Demo

- Go to createai.microbit.org
- Connect/use as radio
- Walk through recording actions
- (get to good stopping point / maybe training the model?)
- Saving your work

## Hands-on

- Get into pairs
- Now your turn!

## Training the model

- Data cleaning - does anything look odd? Replace data samples
- When ready, train the model
- Test the model - does it react as expected? What does it do if still?
- Improve the model (edit data samples, retrain)

## Hands-on

- Now your turn!

## Diversity in Data

- Why is diversity important?
- TBD a slide or two on this, maybe some examples from AI failures - three good points here: https://microbit.org/teach/lessons/strengthening-models-through-adding-diverse-data/

## Hands-on

- Swap and test your micro:bits with another pair
- Did your micro:bit work accurately? How about theirs?

## Discussion

- What did everyone find?
